channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of homicide in shooting death of man in crisis
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy was convicted of criminally negligent homicide Thursday in the shooting death of a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help after his car got stuck in a small mountain community.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Judge declines to halt Trump administration's federal workers layoffs
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday refused to temporarily block the Trump administration’s mass layoff of federal workers while a lawsuit brought by five unions moves forward. U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CEO confidence hits highest point in 3 years: Survey
item.description: Forty-four percent in The Conference Board survey said economic conditions have become more preferable “than six months ago.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Some New York prison guards charged in beating death of handcuffed inmate appear in court
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Six New York prison guards have been indicted for second-degree murder in the beating death of a handcuffed inmate, a brutal incident captured on body-worn cameras that triggered widespread outrage and calls for justice.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at $4B in funding for California high-speed rail
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Trump administration is once again targeting California's controversial high-speed rail project, with federal transportation officials on Thursday announcing an investigation and possible withdrawal of about $4 billion in federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: IRS layoffs could hurt revenue collection and foil efforts to go after rich tax dodgers, experts say
item.description: Experts say the layoffs of roughly 7,000 IRS probationary workers likely mean the end of the agency’s plans to go after high-wealth tax dodgers and could spell disaster for revenue collections.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Smithfield fined $10k after worker locked in oven
item.description: "An employee performing work inside Smokehouse #18 became entrapped after the doors were closed and sealed," reads the citation.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Newton, NewsNation

item.title: Kids' disability rights cases stalled as Trump began to overhaul Education Department
item.description: As President Donald Trump reshaped the Education Department, parents say investigations and mediations into kids' disability rights cases ground to a halt.
item.dc:creator: Heather Hollingsworth, Collin Binkley And Annie Ma, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dr. Mehmet Oz holds millions from companies that he'd wield power over if confirmed, report shows
item.description: Dr. Mehmet Oz holds millions of dollars worth of shares in health insurance, fertility, pharmaceutical and vitamin companies.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz And Brian Slodysko, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: USS Harry S. Truman commanding officer relieved after collision with merchant ship near Suez Canal
item.description: The commanding officer of the U.S. Navy aircraft carrier USS Harry S. Truman was relieved of his duty on Thursday, just over a week after the vessel collided with a merchant ship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The man accused of stabbing Salman Rushdie declines to take the stand as the defense rests
item.description: The New Jersey man on trial in the 2022 stabbing of author Salman Rushdie has declined to testify in his defense.
item.dc:creator: Carolyn Thompson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Funeral held for 2 Detroit kids who died in cold while family gets promise of a home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A group has stepped forward to provide a home for surviving family members of two Detroit children who died in a cold van in a casino parking garage, an official said. A funeral was held Thursday for Darnell Currie Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A flight to a Toronto paramedics conference becomes a real emergency for a former EMT
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former paramedic Peter Carlson is still having difficulty putting into perspective the shuddering jolt he and 79 others aboard Delta Air Lines flight 4819 experienced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bird flu vaccine for chickens given conditional license from USDA
item.description: The company, Zoetis, said this license was granted on the “demonstration of safety, purity, and reasonable expectation of efficacy.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelans migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with layover in Honduras
item.description: Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. have returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls frozen item that 'may contain' plastic, removes product from shelves
item.description: A representative for Trader Joe’s told Nexstar on Thursday that the frozen product is not currently available at stores amid the recall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 23% of Americans ended a relationship over money: Survey
item.description: A new report shows nearly 1 in 4 Americans have ended a relationship because they weren't on the same page when it comes to finances.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Officials investigating bomb threat at Kennedy Center, police say
item.description: The Kennedy Center was evacuated on Thursday after a bomb threat was reported at the venue, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: The science behind snow needles
item.description: When snow falls, the temperature and humidity conditions in the dendritic growth zone, often referred to as the "snow growth zone," help shape these ice crystals.
item.dc:creator: Tommy House, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee couple brings relief to communities hit by Helene
item.description: Sarah and Dennis Rutland volunteered to serve Samaritan's Purse in Valdosta, Georgia where Hurricane Helene caused significant damage to homes and property.
item.dc:creator: Don Dare, NewsNation

item.title: Most worried DOGE cuts could hurt services in their communities: survey
item.description: Most U.S. adults in the latest survey, 62 percent, disagreed with the notion that the president is authorized to terminate federal employees who do not align with his views or agenda.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: What's the key to surviving a plane crash? Skilled crew, strong seats and wings that detach
item.description: After watching videos of a Delta Air Lines jet catch fire upon landing and flip over on a Toronto runway, it's fair to wonder how anyone could have survived.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Russian aircraft fly in Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone, US says
item.description: Russian military aircraft were spotted flying in the Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone (ADIZ) on Tuesday and Wednesday, U.S. officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies. State and local health departments are only just beginning to hear from officials at the CDC, nearly a month after the inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: This rookie QB card could be worth $50K if you pull it from a new Topps set
item.description: Denver Broncos rookie quarterback Bo Nix impressed on the field, and now a Colorado sports card store is ready to pay $50,000 to keep a unique card featuring him close to home.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KRON4

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: The white man who pleaded guilty to shooting a Black teen who rang a wrong doorbell dies
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — An 86-year-old Missouri man has died just days after pleading guilty to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, prosecutors announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Polar vortex makes much of US colder than Greenland, but warmth is coming. Then more cold
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The polar vortex hit its peak across much of America on Wednesday, with an icy grip that made Arctic Greenland seem like a toasty vacation spot in comparison. Even Mars has been warmer than North Dakota this week. But there's hope.
item.dc:creator: BY SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KRON4

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: A new article published this week in The Lancet medical journal found that more than half of the world's population lacks access to safe and affordable medical oxygen services, and most people outside of high-income countries go without adequate oxygen services for their medical conditions.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, ABC 4
